title: Winning Post 10 (2124680)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: **Winning Post 10 2023**
- Steam AppID of the game: **2124680**

## System Information
- GPU:   **Zen 2/RDNA 2 custom AMD APU (steam deck)**
- Video driver version:  **4.6 Compatibility Profile Mesa 22.2.0**
- Kernel version:  **5.13.0-valve36-1-neptune**
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/hmlaa/8bfbb488f023cf0bdfb56722724ed3ef

## I confirm:
- [ yes] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ yes] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

 On Steam Deck no report is generated, the game launches as usual. 

## Symptoms  
Steam deck running GE-Proton 8-13 will not recognize Japanese text input (full width hiragana or katakana) in one specific game, Winning Post 10 2023. I've tried all available Proton versions on the steam deck as well before GE. I've posted and discussed my issue on Stack Exchange, Reddit, Twitter, discord, and also tried many solutions from GitHub.  The fix that worked for previous games was inputting the following code (on Github courtesy of user Xargonwan) into Konsole while the Steam deck is in desktop mode:

#!/bin/bash

sudo steamos-readonly disable

sudo pacman-key --init

sudo pacman-key --populate archlinux

sudo pacman -S glibc

sudo sed -i "s%#ja_JP.UTF-8 UTF-8%ja_JP.UTF-8 UTF-8%" /etc/locale.gen

sudo locale-gen

sudo steamos-readonly enable


Then, I input LANG=ja_JP.UTF-8 %command% in the game's startup options on Steam to enable the locale, however, this solution does not work for this game. It worked for the 2022 version of the same game and for my other Japanese language games. 

https://gist.github.com/hmlaa/6168162b2627c254ff23bb6a0ea719a2

## Reproduction
1. Open Steam gaming mode (also does not work on desktop mode)
2. Select "New Game"
3. Name your character
4. No text populated and an error message is given stating (translated) "Please use full-width Japanese characters. Half-width characters are not supported"

title: The Quarry (1577120)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: The Quarry
- Steam AppID of the game: 1577120

## System Information
- GPU: RX 7900 GRE
- Video driver version: Mesa 25.0.7
- Kernel version: 6.14.9-300.fc42.x86_64
- Link to full system information report: [systeminfo.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20705863/systeminfo.txt)
- Proton version: proton-9.0-4f, experimental-bleeding-edge-10.0

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms 

The game has graphical glitches if SSAO is enabled. There is light or dark blotches flickering like this:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a924fa71-eeab-4a12-8214-3b3108efddc5)
and also sometimes what looks like some texture flickering.

Disabling SSAO in the settings the game appears to be rendered correctly. 
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a011c3b2-2f8a-48aa-9d12-2d928750fb07)

I noticed several reports on ProtonDB mentioning graphical glitches and various workarounds. I tried forcing DX11, Vulkan and LLVM but it made no difference. Only turning off SSAO fixes the issue

Maybe this is a Mesa driver issue, however I did see some reports on ProtonDB from Nvidia users too.

## Reproduction

- Start the game and make sure SSAO is enabled
- Pay attention to the character models, the glitches are more noticeable during some light conditions (they were more noticeable in chapter 2)

[steam-1577120.proton-9.0-4f.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20707087/steam-1577120.proton-9.0-4f.log)
[steam-1577120-experimental-bleeding-edge-10.0.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20707088/steam-1577120-experimental-bleeding-edge-10.0.log)

title: Renegade X
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Renegade X
- Steam AppID of the game: N/A (Not on Steam) - game website is [here](https://totemarts.games/games/renegade-x/)

## System Information
- GPU: RTX 3060 Mobile
- Video driver version: NVIDIA 575
- Kernel version: 6.14
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [https://gist.github.com/Jack-Balcombe/b9fc6dd38d53bf1ea621037dd0c3c141](https://gist.github.com/Jack-Balcombe/b9fc6dd38d53bf1ea621037dd0c3c141)
- Proton version: 10.0-2

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.



```
[2025-09-05 20:15:39.572] [warn]  Running migration for >=1.2.42
[2025-09-05 20:15:39.760] [info]  Totem Arts Launcher (version 1.2.46) ready
[2025-09-05 20:17:52.648] [info]  Totem Arts Launcher (version 1.2.46) ready
[2025-09-05 20:20:33.483] [info]  Totem Arts Launcher (version 1.2.46) ready
```

## Symptoms 
Renegade X worked fine for me with Proton 9, but Proton 10 seems to have broken compatibility with the game. Both the game and its launcher (the Totem Arts Game Centre) do not run at all on Proton 10.

## Reproduction
Attempt to run the Totem Arts Game Centre via Steam using Proton, and observe that it doesn't work.

## I confirm:
- [Yes] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [Yes] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

 (Will add this later)

## Symptoms
No video paths other than VideoLAN work.
VideoLAN has very bad performance compared to Windows.

## Reproduction
-Start Whirligig
-Try playing a video with and without VideoLAN as video path selected

title: Lost Horizon (40350)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Lost Horizon

## System Information

- Video driver version: nvidia 575.64
- Kernel version: 6.15.4

- Proton version: Experimental, Hotfix and 9.0-4

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.



[steam-40350.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20966785/steam-40350.log)

## Symptoms 

When starting the game a small launcher pops up with the options to either play, configure, website, readme or quit. But the options play and configure are greyed out.

## Reproduction

1. Install the game
2. Start the game
3. Launcher appears with greyed out options
